name: Square Spring Roll (Ram Goi)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Raw shrimp, shelled and deveined
2 Scallions, both white and green parts
recipeInstructions:
For dieters or the cholesterol-conscious: an oven-baked version of spring rolls. In Vietnam, these are often baked over charcoal.
1. Cut the shrimp into small pieces and sprinkle with black pepper and sugar.
2. Slice scallions crosswise into very thin slices.
3. Slice pork into thin pieces (3 x 2 x 1/8 inch).
4. Combine half of the sliced scallion with shrimp and meat, fish sauce, and a dash of black pepper in one bowl.
5. Chop garlic finely; place on a platter near the stove, along with remaining scallions.
6. Heat oil in a pan and fry garlic and remaining scallion briefly until they brown slightly.
7. Add pork-shrimp mixture to the pan and keep stirring over high heat for about 5 minutes.
8. Cut or break rice papers into quarters. Place on a flat surface.
9. Using a pastry brush or fingers, paint water over each piece of paper to make them soft and flexible.
10. Fill wrappers with shrimp and pork pieces arranged in a square shape, then fold the pointed end over the filling twice.
11. Fold the sides over and continue rolling into 2-inch-long cylinders about 1 inch thick.
12. Place rolls on a tray with open ends facing down; repeat for remaining rolls.
13. Place rolls directly on the oven rack without preheating (350°F). Bake for about 40 minutes, 20 minutes per side.
Note: Rolls can be filled ahead of time and refrigerated or baked and kept at room temperature for several hours without losing crispness. Use bamboo chopsticks or tongs to turn the rolls.
